question: Is the jukebox now located on the left side of the image (previously on the right)?
Is the neon 'BEST BURGER IN TOWN' sign now positioned on the right side of the back wall?
Is the chalkboard menu now positioned on the left side of the back wall?
Is the man reading the newspaper now positioned on the right side of the image?
Is the text 'BEST BURGER IN TOWN' on the neon sign readable from left to right (not mirror-reversed)?
Is the chalkboard header 'DAILY SPECIALS:' readable and correctly oriented?
Are the menu items on the chalkboard (e.g., 'MEATLOAF', 'CHICKEN POT PIE', 'LIVER & ONIONS') and their prices readable from left to right?
Is the text 'PIE OF THE DAY: APPLE' at the bottom of the chalkboard readable and correctly oriented?
Is the newspaper headline 'LOCAL HERO SAVES CAT' readable and correctly oriented?
Is 'The Times' masthead on the newspaper readable and correctly oriented?
Does the neon sign maintain its original glowing effect and typography style without distortion?
Does the chalkboard text retain its hand-drawn chalk appearance without looking digitally typed or smeared?
Does the corrected text on the neon sign align correctly with the perspective and angle of the wall?
Does the newspaper text align naturally with the curvature of the paper and the man's hands (no floating text)?
Is the image free of visible vertical seams, stitching lines, or disjointed boundaries from the flip?
Is the area surrounding the corrected text elements free from blurring, smudging, or ghosting artifacts?
